top_workflow.problem: Clients with viable cases need a fast match that considers jurisdiction, case type, firm capacity, and client preference.
top_workflow.hypothesis: A transparent matching rubric will raise accepted matches while reducing rework for advocates and firms.
top_workflow.next_decision: Close the weakest gate before committing roadmap capacity.

workflows.problem: Clients with viable cases need a fast match that considers jurisdiction, case type, firm capacity, and client preference.
workflows.hypothesis: A transparent matching rubric will raise accepted matches while reducing rework for advocates and firms.

workflows.problem: Ambiguous quiz answers create downstream case review delays and lower confidence in eligibility recommendations.
workflows.hypothesis: A guided repair loop can improve qualification precision without making intake feel bureaucratic.

workflows.problem: High-need clients can be buried behind lower-risk follow-ups when urgency and readiness are not visible together.
workflows.hypothesis: A triage queue that combines urgency, readiness, and outreach history will improve first-contact speed.

workflows.problem: Some qualified clients cannot be matched immediately because the right firm capacity or jurisdiction fit is unavailable.
workflows.hypothesis: A recovery path with alternate routing and capacity alerts will prevent viable clients from dropping out.

workflows.problem: Firm availability, state coverage, case appetite, and response speed can drift faster than manual routing rules.
workflows.hypothesis: Lightweight firm-side capacity signals will improve match acceptance and reduce dead-end referrals.

workflows.problem: Clients often do not know which documents or medical evidence are needed before a lawyer evaluates the case.
workflows.hypothesis: A plain-language checklist will raise case readiness and reduce repeated advocate follow-up.

workflows.problem: Clients can lose trust after referral if expectations, next steps, and firm response times are unclear.
workflows.hypothesis: A structured handoff timeline will improve client confidence and firm follow-through.

workflows.problem: Clients need reassurance during long benefits timelines, especially when agencies or insurers move slowly.
workflows.hypothesis: Milestone visibility will reduce avoidable support contacts and improve trust through long waits.

workflows.problem: Veterans need claim-specific evidence guidance that differs from disability and workers compensation journeys.
workflows.hypothesis: A vertical-specific readiness checklist can validate expansion demand before deeper workflow investment.

workflows.problem: State rules, deadlines, and firm coverage create routing complexity for injured workers seeking help.
workflows.hypothesis: State-specific guardrails can reduce mismatches while preserving a simple intake journey.

prdCards.user_need: Show why the recommended firm is a fit before the advocate confirms the referral.
prdCards.acceptance_criteria: Match rationale is visible, auditable, and measured before launch review.
prdCards.problem: Firm availability, state coverage, case appetite, and response speed can drift faster than manual routing rules.
prdCards.hypothesis: Lightweight firm-side capacity signals will improve match acceptance and reduce dead-end referrals.
prdCards.user_story: As a partner law firm, I need show why the recommended firm is a fit before the advocate confirms the referral.
prdCards.validation_plan: Review case_readiness_score by case type, state, firm cohort, and advocate team before scaling.

prdCards.user_need: Route low-confidence cases to review instead of presenting a brittle answer.
prdCards.acceptance_criteria: Exception path is visible, auditable, and measured before launch review.

prdCards.hypothesis: A transparent matching rubric will raise accepted matches while reducing rework for advocates and firms.

prdCards.validation_plan: Review match_acceptance_rate by case type, state, firm cohort, and advocate team before scaling.

prdCards.validation_plan: Review client_trust_score by case type, state, firm cohort, and advocate team before scaling.
